description: Ontology defining the metrics, stages, and structural requirements for effective Time-to-Productivity in Engineering teams.

concepts.name: Developer Onboarding
concepts.description: The structured process of integrating a new software engineer into a team, maximizing their autonomy and speed to independent delivery.

concepts.name: Time to First Commit
concepts.description: The time elapsed from day 1 until the developer's first meaningful commit merges to master/main. Target: < 3 days.

concepts.name: Time to First Production PR
concepts.description: The time elapsed until the developer independently pushes a production-ready Pull Request. Target: < 10 days.

concepts.name: Golden Rule Setup
concepts.description: The principle that a new developer should be able to run the entire system locally or in a DevContainer in under 1 hour.

concepts.name: Structured Buddy System
concepts.description: Pairing the new hire with an experienced engineer. Must include structured sessions, checklists, and bidirectional feedback; not just 'ask me anything'.

concepts.name: Contextual Immersion
concepts.description: Weeks 1-2. Focus on architecture overview, logging, deployment procedures, and fixing real but controlled bugs.

concepts.name: Simulated Seniority
concepts.description: Week 4. Testing autonomy through medium-sized features, architectural defenses, and critical retro feedback. Validates hiring success.

concepts.name: Friction Heatmap
concepts.description: Analyzing PR friction rates and modular confusion to dynamically upgrade documentation for future onboardings.
